title: Daily status — backend
description: Read workspace/DONE_CRITERIA.md, then read your brief at briefs/backend.md. Write updates/backend.md in the required format. Flag any cross-team concern via send_message before marking complete. Follow your system prompt exactly.

title: Daily status — frontend
description: Read workspace/DONE_CRITERIA.md, then read your brief at briefs/frontend.md. Write updates/frontend.md in the required format. Flag any cross-team concern via send_message before marking complete. Follow your system prompt exactly.

title: Daily status — qa
description: Read workspace/DONE_CRITERIA.md, then read your brief at briefs/qa.md. Write updates/qa.md in the required format. Flag any cross-team concern via send_message before marking complete. Follow your system prompt exactly.

title: URGENT: Backend clarify release strategy (cutover vs flag)
description:
**Conflict:** Backend is building the full-cutover checklist (DNS flip, read-only toggle, rollback). Frontend and QA are both building for a feature-flag rollout with Monday ramp-up. The release doc says "full cutover Friday," but the eng lead said "flag it and ramp" in the weekly.

**Ask:** Call out the actual release strategy right now. Is this:
- Full cutover Friday (DNS flip, no flag, hard cutover from v1→v2)?
- Feature-flag rollout (flag default off, enable per-user, ramp Monday)?

If you don't have authority to decide, escalate to the eng lead TODAY. This cascades to:
- Frontend's implementation approach (flag vs no flag)
- QA's test plan scope (cutover validation vs ramp validation)
- Infra checklist (cutover readiness vs flag infrastructure)

Confirm the decision by EOD so frontend and QA can finalize their plans.

title: Backend: Confirm coupon endpoint in v2.yaml or commit to adding it today
description:
**Blocker:** Frontend is building the coupon flow against `/v2/coupons/apply` (a mock from last sprint), but cannot find it in v2.yaml. Frontend's message: "Is `/v2/coupons/apply` in the v2 contract? This blocks feature parity for shipping."

**Ask:** 
1. Confirm that `/v2/coupons/apply` exists in `api/v2.yaml` with the schema frontend is building against.
2. If it's not there, commit to adding it TODAY with the discount object schema frontend is expecting.

Reply directly to frontend with the answer — either the exact endpoint path + schema, or "I'm adding it now, ETA 2 hours."

This unblocks frontend's coupon implementation and is a hard requirement for Friday.

title: Backend: Claim staging seed ownership or name the DRI at infra
description:
**Hard blocker:** QA's staging DB seed is broken post-migration — fixture users and promo_codes table are empty. QA cannot run the full regression suite. The seed script is at `infra/seed/` with no owner listed.

QA's message: "Is this backend, infra, or me?"

**Ask:** 
1. Is the staging seed script YOUR domain, or INFRA's?
2. If it's you: commit to fixing it by EOD today. QA needs a healthy DB to run full regression suite before Friday sign-off.
3. If it's infra: name the DRI (directly responsible individual) at infra AND commit to follow up with them in the next 30 minutes.

Reply directly to QA with the answer and ETA for a working seed.

This is a hard blocker for any end-to-end validation before Friday.

title: Backend: Confirm refund endpoint shape is final, ready for QA test writing
description:
**Dependency for QA:** QA has 8 refund regression test cases to write, but is waiting for confirmation that `/v2/refunds` endpoint shape is locked. Your brief says the endpoint is "wired" with "integration tests passing," but QA needs explicit confirmation it's final.

**Ask:** Confirm that the `/v2/refunds` response schema is final and ready for QA to write regression test cases. Reply directly to QA: "Yes, refund endpoint is locked. Write your tests." or "Not quite — it will be ready at [time]."

This unblocks 8 of QA's remaining 22 regression tests.

title: Frontend: Enable checkout_v2 flag for QA test user
description:
**Quick action:** QA needs the `checkout_v2_enabled` flag turned ON for their test user so they can start exploratory testing of the new checkout flow today.

Your brief says the flag is wired and defaults to OFF; QA can be enabled per-user. QA's message: "Can you flip the checkout_v2 flag on for my test user? I need it on to run exploratory testing today."

**Ask:** Enable the flag for QA's test user (check their test user ID in Slack or ask). This unblocks QA's exploratory testing track immediately.

title: Frontend: Drive design to deliver post-refund confirmation mocks + error state copy by Thursday EOD
description:
**Blocker:** Two design deliverables are missing and blocking sign-off:
1. Post-refund confirmation screen mocks (both frontend and QA need it)
2. Final error state copy for declined card, expired session, network timeout (frontend waiting to finalize error states)

Your brief: "Haven't heard back" from design on empty-state mocks.
QA brief: "Can't validate copy without them."

**Situation:** PM is out sick until Thursday. Design responsiveness is slow.

**Ask:** Today, directly reach out to your design contact and escalate these two deliverables as CRITICAL PATH for Friday ship. Need both by Thursday EOD at latest. If design says they can't prioritize, escalate to whoever is covering for PM while they're out.

These are blocking sign-off and QA validation before Friday.

title: Frontend: Provide coupon discount object schema to backend ASAP
description:
**Blocker:** Backend is ready to add `/v2/coupons/apply` endpoint to v2.yaml TODAY, but needs the exact request/response schema from you.

Your brief mentioned you're building the coupon flow against a mock from last sprint. Backend needs to know:
- **Request structure:** What does the request to `/v2/coupons/apply` look like? (coupon code param, user ID, etc.)
- **Response structure:** What does the discount object look like? (discount amount, type, expiry, etc.)

**Ask:** Send backend the exact schema/mock from your implementation within the next 30 minutes. Backend committed to wiring the endpoint and pushing v2.yaml update within 2 hours of receiving your spec.

This unblocks your coupon flow implementation for Friday ship.

title: QA: Provide test user ID to frontend for checkout_v2 flag toggle
description:
**Blocker:** Frontend is ready to enable the `checkout_v2_enabled` feature flag for your test user TODAY, but needs your test user ID.

Your request: "Can you flip the checkout_v2 flag on for my test user? I need it on to run exploratory testing today."

**Ask:** Reply to frontend with your test user ID (or the email/username associated with your test account). Frontend will toggle the flag ON in the flag management system immediately.

This unblocks your exploratory testing of the new checkout flow on v2 API.

note: Provided test user ID (qa-checkout-test-001) to frontend. Flag toggle can proceed. Also updated frontend on endpoint readiness and release strategy clarification from backend.
